GraceUXGuidelines.Audience: Engineers and maintainers operating the vvoc CLI, managed OpenCode agents, skills, and plugins.
GraceUXGuidelines.Guideline: CLI output should be explicit about selected scope, selected config source, writes performed, skipped writes, and follow-up restart instructions.
Invalid existing vvoc configs must fail loudly before mutation and should point users to manual repair plus rerun instructions.
Status and doctor are diagnostic exceptions: they report selected paths and validation problems without normalizing or rewriting config.
Mutating commands must be idempotent and conservative; reruns should report kept, updated, or created state rather than silently clobbering user-owned content.
Workflow prompts and tool results should preserve exact work-item IDs, tracked subagent names, mode, required reviewers, and hard-stop status language.
Review-only workflows report findings and stop before implementation unless the user explicitly asks for fixes.
Plugin toggle UX should distinguish OpenCode plugin registration from vvoc-level enabled or disabled status.
Secret and permission UX should favor bounded safe defaults, visible manual fallback, and no raw secret exposure in logs.
Hashline edit errors should fail loudly on stale anchors and include refreshed nearby anchors when available.
README examples are the user-facing CLI contract and must stay synchronized when command names, flags, setup flow, or config locations change.
